chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI SCRA No. 316 & 318 of 2019 Date Order with Signature of Judge Hearing of case (Priority) 1. For order on office objection 2. For hearing of Main Case 3. For hearing of CMA No. 414/2023 09.02.2026 Mr. Pervaiz Ahmed Memon Advocate for the Applicant Per learned counsel, the impugned order is without any independent discussion or deliberation on the facts and circumstances of the case. Learned counsel demonstrates that the impugned order comprises solely of reproduction culminating in dissonant conclusion. Learned counsel submits that the impugned judgment has been rendered in a perfunctory manner and the same could not be termed as a speaking order. Learned counsel has placed courier receipt as well as tracking record to demonstrate that the service has been effected. The Appellate Tribunal is the last fact-finding forum in the statutory hierarchy; therefore, it is incumbent upon it to render independent deliberations and findings on each issue. The manner in which the appeals in general are to be addressed has been emphasized by the Supreme Court in the judgment reported as 2019 SCMR 1726. This High Court has consistently maintained that the Appellate Tribunal is required to proffer independent reasons and findings, and in the absence thereof a perfunctory order could not be sustained. Reliance is placed on the judgment dated 02.10.2024 in SCRA 1113 of 2023 and judgment dated 27.08.2024 in SCRA 757 of 2015. Earlier Division Bench judgments have also maintained that if the impugned order is discrepant in the manner as aforesaid, the correct course is to remand the matter for adjudication afresh.

Reliance is placed on the judgment dated 02.10.2024 in SCRA 1113 of 2023 and judgment dated 27.08.2024 in SCRA 757 of 2015. Earlier Division Bench judgments have also maintained that if the impugned order is discrepant in the manner as aforesaid, the correct course is to remand the matter for adjudication afresh. Reliance is placed on the judgment dated 10.12.2024 in ITRA 343 of 2024. We are of the considered view that the impugned order could not be considered to be a speaking order and is prima facie devoid of any independent reasoning etc. The entire judgment comprises essentially of reproduction and is crowned with a dissonant conclusion. Hence, no case is set forth to sustain the impugned order, which is hereby set aside and the matter is remanded back to the Appellate Tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law. Reference applications are disposed of. Office to place copy of this order in connected matter. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969. Judge Judge Amjad PS
